issues.summary: ExploreResponseAssembler sits in api/ but has no HTTP state — it is a pure model-to-DTO translator with only static methods, and would be better organized in api/model/ or a dedicated assembler sub-package
evidence: ExploreResponseAssembler is final with all static methods and no HttpExchange reference; it has no reason to share a package with ConcordanceHandlers or SketchHandlers
The class constructs api/model/*.java response objects from model/exploration/*.java domain objects — it belongs with the model it produces
issues.suggestion: Move ExploreResponseAssembler to api/model/ (or api/assembler/) to keep the api/ package focused on HTTP concerns (handlers, routing, error handling). Update package declaration and imports.

issues.summary: ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry and ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry are parallel nested records representing the same graph-edge concept with identical fields (source, target, log_dice, type), defined redundantly in two sibling response classes
evidence: ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry: source, target, log_dice (double), type (RelationEdgeType)
ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry: source, target, log_dice (double), type (RelationEdgeType)
Both records appear in JSON responses with identical field shapes and the same RelationEdgeType enum values
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared EdgeEntry record into api/model/ (or reuse the domain model Edge class) and reference it from both ComparisonResponse and ExploreResponse. Alternatively keep the duplication and document the intentional divergence.

issues.summary: SeedCollocateEntry is a top-level public record in api/model/ while CollocateProfileEntry, CoreCollocateEntry, DiscoveredNounEntry and EdgeEntry are nested private records inside their owning response class — inconsistent nesting strategy across sibling files
evidence: SeedCollocateEntry is a standalone public record in api/model/
DiscoveredNounEntry, CoreCollocateEntry, EdgeEntry, Parameters are all nested inside ExploreResponse
CollocateProfileEntry and EdgeEntry are nested inside ComparisonResponse
SeedCollocateEntry is imported from api/model.ExploreResponse.java as a top-level type while sibling entry types are nested
issues.suggestion: Decide one strategy: either make all sub-entry records nested (private) inside their owning response class, or promote all of them to top-level records in api/model/. The nested approach is more common in Java record-based response models.

issues.summary: SketchHandlers.executeRelationQueries() silently collects per-relation IOExceptions into an errors array and returns HTTP 200, while all exploration handlers propagate any IOException to HttpApiUtils.wrapWithErrorHandling — two different failure strategies within the same HTTP handler layer
evidence: SketchHandlers line ~148: catch (IOException e) { logger.warn(...); relationErrors.add(...); } — error is swallowed into the result
ExplorationHandlers handlers all declare throws IOException and let HttpApiUtils.wrapWithErrorHandling map it to 500
A client calling /api/sketch/{lemma} receives HTTP 200 with an errors field on partial failure; a client calling /api/semantic-field/explore receives HTTP 500 on any failure
issues.suggestion: Document the intentional difference explicitly in both SketchHandlers and ExplorationHandlers so future contributors understand why the two strategies coexist. Consider whether exploration endpoints should also support partial failure semantics if individual relations can be independently retried.

issues.summary: HttpApiUtils.wrapWithErrorHandling explicitly catches NullPointerException and maps it to HTTP 500, conflating programming errors with recoverable application failures and masking potential bugs
evidence: } catch (NullPointerException | IllegalStateException e) { logger.error(...); sendError(exchange, 500, ...) }
NullPointerException is a programming error in Java; catching it at an HTTP boundary hides the bug rather than surfacing it
issues.suggestion: Remove NullPointerException from the catch block and let it propagate to the outermost catch (Exception e) handler, or fix the underlying null-safety issues using @NonNull annotations and NullAway/JSpecify enforcement. Catching NPE as a first-class error type is a sign that null safety is not enforced by the type system.

issues.summary: executeCollocations and executeSurfacePattern are parallel collocate-lookup methods on QueryExecutor but use inconsistent naming grammar — one is verb+noun-plural, the other is verb+noun-phrase
evidence: QueryExecutor declares: executeCollocations(lemma, cqlPattern, minLogDice, maxResults) and executeSurfacePattern(bcqlPattern, minLogDice, maxResults)
Both return List<WordSketchResult> ranked by logDice
The plural 'Collocations' implies multiple operations or a result set; 'SurfacePattern' describes the mechanism, not the output
issues.suggestion: Rename to align grammar: either executeCollocations / executeSurfacePatternCollocations, or executeSimpleCollocates / executeSurfacePatternCollocates. The key is that both names should indicate they return ranked collocates and hint at the lookup mechanism.

issues.summary: GroupStats (inner record in BlackLabQueryExecutor) is a generic name that does not communicate what is being grouped or what statistics it holds
evidence: private record GroupStats(Map<String,Long> freqMap, Map<String,String> lemmaPosMap) — name does not describe that these are collocate-frequency and collocate-pos mappings derived from BlackLab hit groups
Contrast with SeedCollocateData, AggregatedCollocateStats in MultiSeedExplorer — those names communicate what the records contain
issues.suggestion: Rename to CollocateFrequencyData or HitGroupStats to communicate that this holds collocate-frequency and POS mappings aggregated from BlackLab hit groups.

issues.summary: ExploreResponseAssembler is a final class with only static methods that perform single-hop property mapping from domain objects to DTO records — it is a utility dumping ground that encapsulates no policy, enforces no invariants, and reduces no meaningful duplication
evidence: All 10+ methods are static. The class has a private constructor and no state.
Methods like buildDiscoveredNounEntries, buildCoreCollocateEntries, buildEdgeEntries each iterate a list and call a constructor — they are one-to-one mappings
No method enforces shared invariants (e.g. sorting order, field truncation, normalization) that would justify centralizing the logic
issues.suggestion: Evaluate whether the translation methods provide enough value to justify a dedicated class. If the handlers called the DTO constructors directly (or the domain model objects were directly serializable via Jackson), this class would not be needed. At minimum, consider replacing it with a static factory method on each response record, keeping the mapping logic adjacent to the type it constructs.

issues.summary: SharedExploreParams (package-private record in api/) carries the same three fields as ExplorationOptions (domain model) with identical semantics, creating a permanent translation layer between the two with no additional policy
evidence: SharedExploreParams: topCollocates, minShared, logDiceThreshold
ExplorationOptions: topCollocates, minShared, logDiceThreshold — identical field names and semantics
ExplorationHandlers parses HTTP params into SharedExploreParams, then constructs ExplorationOptions from it — two objects are created that carry the same data
issues.suggestion: Consider whether SharedExploreParams can be eliminated by having the HTTP parsing code directly construct ExplorationOptions. If HTTP-layer concerns (e.g. default values, parameter name aliases) require separation, document the rationale so the duplication is intentional.

issues.summary: RelationUtils and RelationPatternUtils are two separate static-utility classes in config/ with no instances or state; their methods operate on the same RelationConfig type and could be merged or moved onto RelationConfig itself
evidence: RelationUtils: resolveRelationAlias, validateAliases, findBestCollocatePattern — all take GrammarConfig or String arguments
RelationPatternUtils: buildFullPattern, buildCollocateReversePattern, computeCollocatePosGroup — all take RelationConfig arguments
Both classes are final with private constructors and only static methods
Callers must import both classes when working with relations, with no clear rule about which class to consult first
issues.suggestion: Merge RelationPatternUtils into RelationConfig as instance methods (since all methods already receive a RelationConfig as their first argument), and keep only cross-config utilities in RelationUtils. This reduces the number of utility classes and makes RelationConfig a richer value object.

issues.summary: BlackLab 4.x not published to Maven Central; requires manual local-install script and a raw-GitHub-URL fallback with no SLA
evidence: pom.xml repositories block: 'BlackLab 4.x is NOT published to Maven Central (latest on Central is 3.0.1). REQUIRED FIRST STEP: run ./install-blacklab.sh'
blacklab-github-fallback profile uses 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/…' with checksumPolicy=warn and comment 'DO NOT rely on it in production CI'
issues.suggestion: Mirror the BlackLab 4.x JARs to a private registry (Nexus, Artifactory, or GitHub Packages) so the build is reproducible without an out-of-band install step and without depending on a raw GitHub URL.

issues.summary: jackson-core pinned explicitly alongside the jackson-bom import, which already manages the version
evidence: pom.xml declares jackson-bom 2.17.2 via dependencyManagement import, then also declares jackson-core 2.17.2 as a direct dependency with the same version
issues.suggestion: Remove the explicit jackson-core dependency declaration; the BOM import already pins all Jackson artifacts to a consistent version. The comment 'Pin jackson-core to match databind; another transitive dep resolves an older version otherwise' suggests this was a workaround — verify whether the transitive conflict still exists with the current BOM.

issues.summary: MultiSeedExplorer.findCollocateIntersection performs a redundant extra pass converting List<WordSketchResult> to Map<String,Double> after aggregation is already complete
evidence: Lines after aggregateCollocateStats() build perSeedCollocates by iterating over data.seedCollocateMap() a second time — the same collection already traversed inside fetchCollocatesPerSeed and aggregateCollocateStats
The conversion (List<WordSketchResult> → Map<String,Double>) discards the WordSketchResult type without any filtering or transformation logic
issues.suggestion: Build perSeedCollocates inside fetchCollocatesPerSeed as a parallel Map<String,Map<String,Double>>, or return it directly from SeedCollocateData alongside the existing seedCollocateMap, eliminating the post-aggregation loop.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern passes a long multi-branch inline lambda that requires a sentinel comment to explain the -1 value, making the call-site hard to read
evidence: The lambda passed to collectFrequenciesAndPosFromGroups contains three distinct branches guarded by a 'Uses < 0 to handle any negative sentinel value' comment and two fallback paths (XML parse then plain-text fallback)
The sentinel -1 meaning ('label 2: absent from pattern') is established in CqlUtils.findLabelTokenIndex but must be remembered at the call site
issues.suggestion: Extract the lambda body into a named private method (e.g. extractCollocateFromIdentity(identity, collocatePos)) that encapsulates the sentinel check and fallback logic, removing the need for an inline comment explaining the control flow.

issues.summary: ExploreResponse.primarySeeds() interface method serialises under different JSON keys in each variant, making the seam between the interface and its consumers asymmetric
evidence: ExploreResponse Javadoc: 'Each concrete variant carries its own @JsonProperty annotation; the interface intentionally omits one to avoid inheriting a misleading key name'
SingleSeed serialises the list as 'discovered_nouns'; MultiSeed serialises it as 'source_seeds' — callers of the interface cannot know which JSON key is used without downcasting
issues.suggestion: Split the shared payload into two clearly named accessors on the interface (discoveredNouns() and sourceSeeds()) with default implementations returning empty, or model the two variants as fully separate response types rather than implementations of a shared interface, removing the ambiguity at the seam.

issues.summary: ExploreResponse.Parameters is embedded in ComparisonResponse, coupling a type from the explore-endpoint DTO into the compare-endpoint response
evidence: ExploreResponseAssembler.buildComparisonResponse creates ExploreResponse.Parameters to embed in ComparisonResponse

ComparisonResponse is a distinct endpoint response yet its 'parameters' field carries ExploreResponse.Parameters with nouns_per that is null/omitted — the field makes no semantic sense for a comparison response
issues.suggestion: Define a shared SharedParams record in the api.model package (or a simpler inline record in ComparisonResponse) that contains only the fields relevant to both endpoints (relation, top, min_shared, min_logdice), rather than reusing ExploreResponse.Parameters which carries explore-specific fields.

issues.summary: HttpApiUtils.warnIfWildcardCors has a multi-paragraph Javadoc that restates the implementation logic visible a few lines below
evidence: Javadoc reads: 'Wildcard CORS must be an explicit opt-in: set the system property allow.wildcard.cors=true to suppress the error and allow all origins. Without that property, a wildcard origin fails fast to prevent accidental exposure in production.'
The method body (8 lines) performs exactly what the Javadoc describes: checks System.getProperty('allow.wildcard.cors'), branches on true/false, throws with the same error message as in the Javadoc
issues.suggestion: Trim the Javadoc to a single sentence describing intent ('Fails fast if CORS allow-origin is set to wildcard without explicit opt-in.'), removing the inline re-statement of the branching logic.

issues.summary: CollocateQueryHelper constructor has a multi-sentence Javadoc explaining null-index semantics that is longer than the constructor implementation itself
evidence: Constructor body: 1 line (this.index = index). Javadoc: 4 sentences explaining why null is accepted, who may pass null, and which methods must not be called when null — essentially restating the @Nullable annotation already present on the parameter
The same null semantics are repeated in BlackLabQueryExecutor's package-private constructor Javadoc
issues.suggestion: Replace with a single-line comment: '// null allowed only in test subclasses that override I/O methods'. The @Nullable annotation already communicates the contract; the prose adds no new information.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor interface accumulates multi-paragraph implementation notes and 'Consumer interface audit' prose that belongs in architecture documentation, not in an interface Javadoc
evidence: The interface Javadoc contains a 'Consumer interface audit' section listing which handler class uses which port — this is structural documentation that goes stale when handlers change and is not useful at the call site
It also contains implementation notes ('BlackLab's search() can throw undocumented RuntimeExceptions…') that belong in BlackLabQueryExecutor, not the interface
issues.suggestion: Move the consumer audit table to a package-info.java or architecture doc. Restrict the interface Javadoc to the contract (what each method does, pre-/post-conditions, exceptions), not which classes implement or consume it.

issues.summary: Stale TODO in WordSketchApiServer referencing a potential future migration to Undertow or Jetty with no owner or deadline
evidence: Line 54: '// TODO: migrate to Undertow or Jetty if a multi-module build or custom classloader is needed.'
Same conditional note also appears as a compiler-plugin comment in pom.xml — two copies of the same future-state reminder
issues.suggestion: Either track this as a GitHub issue and remove the TODO comment, or rephrase as a decision record explaining the deliberate choice to use jdk.httpserver (zero deps, loopback-only, fits current scope) rather than framing it as an outstanding migration task.

issues.summary: @NonNull seeds parameter in exploreMultiSeed() contradicted by explicit null-check in body
evidence: Method signature: public @NonNull ExplorationResult exploreMultiSeed(@NonNull Set<String> seeds, ...)
Line 207: if (seeds == null || seeds.size() < 2) — null branch can never execute under the declared contract, creating dead-code confusion
issues.suggestion: Remove the null branch and keep only the size check: if (seeds.size() < 2). If null input is a real concern, drop the @NonNull annotation and document the null behaviour.

issues.summary: CollocateQueryHelper accepts null index with a verbal-only behavioural contract that the type system cannot enforce
evidence: Constructor: CollocateQueryHelper(@Nullable BlackLabIndex index) — Javadoc says 'Callers must not invoke any method that delegates to blackLabIndex when those are null'
BlackLabQueryExecutor test constructor uses @SuppressWarnings("NullAway") to silence the resulting warning
getTotalFrequency, performCollocateSearch all dereference index unconditionally after accepting it as @Nullable
issues.suggestion: Replace the nullable-index test pattern with a dedicated test subclass or a mock that overrides I/O methods. The production constructor should take a non-null index and the @Nullable annotation should be removed.

issues.summary: collocatePos integer used as both an XML attribute-count index and a plain-text word index in executeSurfacePattern, with non-obvious equivalence
evidence: Line 256: int collocatePos = CqlUtils.findLabelTokenIndex(bcqlPattern, 2)
Line 269: extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition(identity, collocatePos) — used as 1-based lemma-attribute index in XML
Line 273: extractPlainTextTokenAt(identity, collocatePos) — same integer reused as 1-based word index in plain text
Comment says 'collocatePos is reused as a 1-based word index (same integer value, different counting scheme)'
issues.suggestion: Introduce separate variables (e.g. collocateAttrIndex vs collocateTokenIndex) or a wrapper that encodes both interpretations, making the dual counting schemes explicit rather than relying on accidental numeric equivalence.

issues.summary: executeCqlQuery() wraps all RuntimeException as IOException, potentially masking NullPointerException and other programming errors as infrastructure failures
evidence: catch (RuntimeException e) { throw new IOException("Unexpected error executing CQL query: " + e.getMessage(), e); }
Comment: 'BlackLab's search() can throw undocumented RuntimeExceptions on index corruption' — but the catch is not scoped to corruption signals; it catches everything including NPE
issues.suggestion: Catch a narrower type if BlackLab exposes one, or at minimum re-throw NullPointerException and other clearly-programming-error exceptions before the catch-all, so genuine bugs surface immediately rather than being swallowed as I/O errors.

issues.summary: @NonNull annotation on exploreMultiSeed seeds parameter is contradicted by null-defensive body, making the nullability contract unreliable
evidence: Parameter declaration: @NonNull Set<String> seeds
Body line 207: if (seeds == null || seeds.size() < 2) — null branch violates the declared annotation
ExplorationService interface also declares @NonNull seeds — the implementation weakens the contract silently
issues.suggestion: Align annotation and implementation: either remove @NonNull and document null behaviour, or remove the null branch and trust the annotation. NullAway or similar tooling should enforce consistency.

issues.summary: WordSketchResult record fields lemma and pos carry no nullability annotations; pos has a documented UNKNOWN_POS sentinel suggesting null is possible but not annotated
evidence: public record WordSketchResult(String lemma, String pos, long frequency, double logDice, double relativeFrequency, List<String> examples)
public static final String UNKNOWN_POS = "unknown" — sentinel implies pos can be absent, but no @Nullable annotation to signal this to callers or tools
issues.suggestion: Annotate lemma and pos with @NonNull or @Nullable as appropriate. If UNKNOWN_POS is used as a stand-in for missing data, @NonNull is correct and the Javadoc should state it. If null is also possible, add @Nullable.

issues.summary: HttpApiUtils imports ExplorationException from exploration.spi to drive HTTP 503 mapping, coupling a general-purpose utility to a specific subsystem
evidence: import pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration.spi.ExplorationException; in HttpApiUtils.java
wrapWithErrorHandling() catches ExplorationException and maps to 503 — this is the only exploration-layer import in the api utilities package
HttpApiUtils is otherwise a generic HTTP utility with no other domain dependencies
issues.suggestion: Move the ExplorationException → 503 mapping to ExplorationHandlers.wrapWithErrorHandling or a dedicated handler wrapper, keeping HttpApiUtils free of exploration-layer imports. Alternatively, introduce a marker interface (e.g. ServiceUnavailableException) in the api layer that ExplorationException can extend.

issues.summary: No CI pipeline: tests only run manually, no automated execution on commits
evidence: The .github/workflows/ directory does not exist — confirmed by filesystem check
39 test files covering core logic, API handlers, integration, and exploration exist but are never automatically triggered
Integration tests requiring a real index (BlackLabQueryExecutorIntegrationTest, BlackLabConllUIndexerTest) use Assumptions.assumeTrue but are never run in any automated context
issues.suggestion: Add a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) that runs 'mvn test' on push and pull_request. For integration tests requiring a real index, either add a small sample index as a test fixture or keep them in a separate Maven profile that CI skips.

issues.summary: Sibling ExplorationService methods use different options types (SingleSeedExplorationOptions vs ExplorationOptions) without documented rationale
evidence: exploreByRelation takes SingleSeedExplorationOptions while exploreMultiSeed takes the base ExplorationOptions
SingleSeedExplorationOptions adds a nounsPerSeed field over the base ExplorationOptions, but this difference is not explained at the interface level
A caller inspecting the interface must understand the class hierarchy to know which options type to construct for which method
issues.suggestion: Document at the ExplorationService interface level why exploreByRelation requires SingleSeedExplorationOptions (the extra nounsPerSeed field only applies to single-seed mode). Alternatively, add a factory method or builder that makes the distinction visible at the call site.

issues.summary: ExploreResponseAssembler.ExamplesContext uses a @Nullable Boolean as a tri-state fallback flag
evidence: ExamplesContext record has field: @Nullable Boolean fallback — null means 'not a fallback', true means 'fallback was used'
Call site in ConcordanceHandlers passes fallback ? Boolean.TRUE : null, which is semantically Optional<Boolean>
This pattern forces callers to box a primitive boolean and treat null as a meaningful third value
issues.suggestion: Replace @Nullable Boolean fallback with boolean fallback (primitive, defaults to false) or with Optional<String> fallbackReason if the reason matters to callers. This eliminates the null-as-false semantic and makes the intent explicit.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor (open disk resource) is constructed before its shutdown hook is registered in Main
evidence: Line: QueryExecutor executor = new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath); — opens the index
Several lines later: Runtime.getRuntime().addShutdownHook(new Thread(() -> { ... executor.close(); ... }));
If a SIGTERM arrives in the window between executor construction and hook registration, the index is left unclosed
issues.suggestion: Register the shutdown hook immediately after executor construction and before any further initialization that could fail. Alternatively, use a try-with-resources block or a single AtomicReference<QueryExecutor> with a hook registered at startup that closes whatever is currently held.

issues.summary: BCQL endpoint audit log is SLF4J INFO, not a structured or dedicated audit trail
evidence: logger.info("BCQL audit – src={} len={} query: {}", ...) — uses the application logger at INFO level
Audit entries intermix with ordinary application logs; no structured format, no dedicated appender
The /api/bcql endpoint accepts arbitrary corpus queries — if the loopback restriction were ever relaxed, audit trail quality would be inadequate
issues.suggestion: Use a named Logger (e.g., LoggerFactory.getLogger("audit.bcql")) so that audit entries can be independently routed in logback.xml. Add a comment noting that the loopback-only bind is the primary access control and the audit log is a secondary observability aid.

issues.summary: Response assemblers (ExploreResponseAssembler, SketchResponseAssembler) are mixed with HTTP handlers in the api/ package, blurring handler vs assembly responsibility
evidence: api/ contains both HTTP handler classes (ExplorationHandlers, SketchHandlers, etc.) and response assembler classes (ExploreResponseAssembler, SketchResponseAssembler)
Assemblers perform data mapping/transformation logic unrelated to HTTP concerns; they share the package namespace with request routing code
issues.suggestion: Move assemblers to a dedicated api/assembler/ subpackage to separate HTTP routing from response construction. This would make the api/ package purely about handling requests and routing, while api/assembler/ handles DTO construction.
